markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Label claims as source, derived, model-suggested, operator-approved, deprecated, superseded, or unsupported. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: ClaimAuthorityLabels leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ClaimAuthorityLabels is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91c204e6; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-125 (ClaimAuthorityLabels) requires labeling claims/facts as source/derived/model_suggested/operator_approved/deprecated/superseded/unsupported with an enforced legal transition table. The code delivers exactly this: a 7-variant enum, a DB CHECK constraint over all 7 labels, a real legal-transition table (operator-approved cannot silently downgrade; unsupported cannot become stable retrieval authority), a guarded mutation returning a typed Conflict on illegal transitions, and a read-side retrieval-trust classifier. Positive and negative paths are proven against real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs:548-556 defines MemoryClaimAuthorityLabel with all 7 MT-named variants (Source, Derived, ModelSuggested, OperatorApproved, Deprecated, Superseded, Unsupported); as_str/from_db (559-584) map them to the exact spec strings.

knowledge_memory.rs:591-616 can_transition_to is a real, non-trivial legal transition table: OperatorApproved can only move to Deprecated/Superseded/Unsupported (never silently downgrade to a weaker source label); Unsupported cannot re-enter live retrieval authority (only Deprecated/Superseded); live labels can be promoted/retired. This directly implements the MT's enforced-transition requirement.

knowledge_memory.rs:801-828 set_memory_fact_authority_label reads the current label, rejects illegal transitions with typed StorageError::Conflict, and only then performs the guarded UPDATE on knowledge_memory_facts. Real durable-state mutation, not a stub.

migration 0241_knowledge_memory_facts.sql:57-59 enforces the authority_label column with a CHECK over the 7 labels, default 'model_suggested' (matches a freshly extracted fact). DB-level + app-level double guard.

tests/knowledge_memory_facts_tests.rs:145-217 authority_label_transition_table_is_enforced runs against real managed PostgreSQL (MemoryFixture::setup -> knowledge_pg, pool_for connects an isolated PG schema; SKIPs loudly when PG binaries absent) and proves model_suggested->operator_approved (legal), operator_approved->model_suggested (illegal, asserts StorageError::Conflict), operator_approved->deprecated (legal). Both positive and negative paths exercised on real PG.

tests/knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:8-9 declares 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks.' — sub_rule_2 satisfied: the MemoryFact backs 1:1 onto a real knowledge_claims row, aligning with Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 claim-lifecycle authority.

src/knowledge_memory/claim_labels.rs:22-40 is_retrieval_trusted / is_retired_label encode the spec rule that probationary/unsupported material must not be served as stable retrieval fact; no escapes/placeholders found anywhere in the MT-125 reachable code.

Aside (non-blocking): the commit message for 91c204e6 lists MT-125, but claim_labels.rs was actually committed earlier in 98beb6d7 with the ontology layer; the label storage type + mutation live in storage::knowledge_memory.rs. This is commit-attribution bookkeeping only and does not affect the code's correctness or runtime proof.
evidence_checked: git show 91c204e6 + git log --grep MT-125 + git log claim_labels.rs (-> 98beb6d7)
grep for deferred-live escapes across MT-125 reachable code

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
